Morningrise (Abbey Road Remaster)

by Opeth

supported by
Blunt Force Trauma
Blunt Force Trauma EVERY SONG IS A PERFECT MASTERPIECE!!!
PJ Brown
PJ Brown This was my first Opeth album, bought 23 years ago in Boston. I love how they evolved over time, but I very much still enjoy their avant garde death metal prog roots. Brilliant 2nd album.

about

Candlelight Records UK London, UK

Premier UK Extreme Metal label whose roster over the years has included Emperor, Opeth, Corrosion of Conformity, Anaal Nathrakh, Enslaved, 1349, Insomnium, Abigail Williams, Obituary, Absu, Onslaught, Limbonic Art, Nachtmystium, Daylight Dies, Orange Goblin, Ihsahn, Crowbar, Xerath, Zyklon, Glorior Belli, Defiance, Myrkskog, October File, Pantheon I, Omnium Gatherum and many more amazing artists. ... more

If you like Morningrise (Abbey Road Remaster), you may also like:

  • Still Life (Remastered)
    by Opeth

    supported by 74 fans who also own “Morningrise (Abbey Road Remaster)”

    I was physically removed from an Opeth Concert by Security in Edmonton, Alberta for trying to start a pit during Serenity Painted Death. ...2010 @ Reds. Still one of my Favorite Opeth songs. I have been listening to this album since it came out. 26+ years ago, one you can put down and come back to and get right into. such a great album. Opeth and their unique take on everything. fantastic song writing and the way they can create a mood or force an atmosphere on the listener.. unmatched. Rat Bastard

    go to album

  • Cutting the Throat of God
    by Ulcerate

    supported by 47 fans who also own “Morningrise (Abbey Road Remaster)”

    Thrashing temperature gradients of shrill dissonance and surreal melody converge into an utterly rapturous cacophony. The dread of totality is self-realizing. mozfor

    go to album

  • Exul
    by Ne Obliviscaris

    supported by 47 fans who also own “Morningrise (Abbey Road Remaster)”

    This is the best album I've heard in years, no joke, it's that good. The atmosphere, the guitar solos, and the violin are perfect. Andre Marques

    go to album
